title: spawn_blocking for RSA-PSS only — not all signing ops — intentional asymmetry
description: Ed25519 and P-256 sign on the async thread; RSA-PSS uses spawn_blocking. A future contributor might ask why not be consistent and offload all three. The asymmetry is documented and correct: Ed25519 ~10µs and P-256 ~100µs are safe on the executor; RSA-2048 PSS ~1-3ms is not. The comment in the code gives the reasoning. The asymmetry should not be "fixed" by either removing spawn_blocking for RSA (regression) or adding it for Ed25519/P-256 (unnecessary overhead and complexity).
